variance_decomposition.definition: The density, speed, and overall tempo of spoken dialogue or narration in the scene.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: characters speaking rapidly and overlapping; a fast-paced, witty banter sequence; a rapid-fire monologue or voiceover; characters interrupting each other constantly; a high volume of spoken words in a short period
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on the described speed, density, and frequency of spoken words, dialogue exchanges, or voiceovers.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score non-verbal sounds (like groans or screams) or written text shown on screen.

variance_decomposition.definition: The density, speed, and overall tempo of spoken dialogue or narration in the scene.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: characters speaking rapidly and overlapping; a fast-paced, witty banter sequence; a rapid-fire monologue or voiceover; characters interrupting each other constantly; a high volume of spoken words in a short period
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on the described speed, density, and frequency of spoken words, dialogue exchanges, or voiceovers.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score non-verbal sounds (like groans or screams) or written text shown on screen.

variance_decomposition.definition: The density, speed, and overall tempo of spoken dialogue or narration in the scene.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: characters speaking rapidly and overlapping; a fast-paced, witty banter sequence; a rapid-fire monologue or voiceover; characters interrupting each other constantly; a high volume of spoken words in a short period
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on the described speed, density, and frequency of spoken words, dialogue exchanges, or voiceovers.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score non-verbal sounds (like groans or screams) or written text shown on screen.

variance_decomposition.definition: The presence and intensity of emotional inflection, tone, pitch, and volume in spoken words (e.g., whispering in fear, shouting in anger, trembling voice).
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A voice trembling with suppressed tears; A furious, screaming argument; A tense, hushed whisper in the dark; A sarcastic, mocking tone of voice
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on descriptions of how words are spoken, focusing on tone, volume, trembling, screaming, whispering, and emotional inflection.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score the semantic content of the words themselves, only the acoustic and emotional delivery of the speech.

variance_decomposition.definition: The visual prominence of a character actively speaking, where their mouth movements, facial expressions, and lip shapes accompany their spoken words.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: Extreme close-up of a character's mouth as they speak; A character speaking directly to the camera; Clear, unobstructed view of a character's face during a monologue; Intense face-to-face dialogue with visible lip movements
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on descriptions of close-ups, face-to-face framing, and clear visibility of the mouth and lips during speech.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score off-screen voices, voiceovers, or scenes where the speaker's face is obscured or turned away.

variance_decomposition.definition: The presence and intensity of emotional inflection, tone, pitch, and volume in spoken words (e.g., whispering in fear, shouting in anger, trembling voice).

variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on descriptions of how words are spoken, focusing on tone, volume, trembling, screaming, whispering, and emotional inflection.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score the semantic content of the words themselves, only the acoustic and emotional delivery of the speech.

variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score off-screen voices, voiceovers, or scenes where the speaker's face is obscured or turned away.

variance_decomposition.definition: The density, speed, and overall tempo of spoken dialogue or narration in the scene.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: characters speaking rapidly and overlapping; a fast-paced, witty banter sequence; a rapid-fire monologue or voiceover; characters interrupting each other constantly; a high volume of spoken words in a short period
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on the described speed, density, and frequency of spoken words, dialogue exchanges, or voiceovers.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score non-verbal sounds (like groans or screams) or written text shown on screen.

variance_decomposition.definition: The presence and intensity of emotional inflection, tone, pitch, and volume in spoken words (e.g., whispering in fear, shouting in anger, trembling voice).
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A voice trembling with suppressed tears; A furious, screaming argument; A tense, hushed whisper in the dark; A sarcastic, mocking tone of voice
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on descriptions of how words are spoken, focusing on tone, volume, trembling, screaming, whispering, and emotional inflection.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score the semantic content of the words themselves, only the acoustic and emotional delivery of the speech.

variance_decomposition.definition: The re-entry of an established character after an absence, requiring the viewer to retrieve and match their facial identity.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: character returns; re-entering the scene; recognizing a familiar face; reunion of characters; character steps back into frame
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on the return of known characters and the narrative/visual weight of their re-entry.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score high for characters who have been continuously on screen without leaving.

variance_decomposition.definition: The tracking of characters' physical movements, orientation, and navigation relative to their immediate environment.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A character walking through a doorway or hallway; A character turning, climbing, or ducking to avoid obstacles; Descriptions of a character's physical path through a space; A character orienting themselves towards a specific landmark
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for action verbs of motion and direction (e.g., 'walked past', 'turned left', 'climbed up', 'navigated through') and spatial prepositions.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score stationary characters or general travel over long distances (like driving a car on a highway) where local navigation is not described.

variance_decomposition.definition: The extent to which characters must navigate, comply with, or enforce explicit, formal rules, laws, regulations, or operational protocols.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A courtroom trial with strict legal procedures and objections; A military or tactical operation governed by strict protocols and chain of command; Characters playing a game or sport with explicit, rigid rules; A character navigating a highly bureaucratic system or security checkpoint
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for explicit mentions of laws, regulations, protocols, checklists, game rules, or bureaucratic procedures.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score implicit social norms or moral choices unless they are codified into formal, explicit rules or laws.

variance_decomposition.definition: The degree to which the viewer must infer what a character believes to be true, or what knowledge they possess or lack, especially when their beliefs are false or outdated.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A character acting on a false belief; A character realizing they were mistaken; A character searching for information they lack
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for descriptions of characters acting on incomplete information, expressing mistaken assumptions, or discovering new facts.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score simple emotional reactions or physical actions that do not require tracking what a character knows or believes.

variance_decomposition.definition: Updating the situational frame of reference during scene cuts, transitions, or sudden shifts in setting, environment, or context.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A sudden cut to a completely new location and set of characters.; A rapid transition from an indoor to an outdoor setting.; A sudden shift in the narrative context (e.g., waking up from a dream).
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for text describing scene cuts, transitions, shifts in setting, changes in the cast of characters present, or sudden changes in the environmental context.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score movement within a single, continuous shot or location unless it involves a radical shift in the situational context.

variance_decomposition.definition: The density, speed, and overall tempo of spoken dialogue or narration in the scene.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: characters speaking rapidly and overlapping; a fast-paced, witty banter sequence; a rapid-fire monologue or voiceover; characters interrupting each other constantly; a high volume of spoken words in a short period
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on the described speed, density, and frequency of spoken words, dialogue exchanges, or voiceovers.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score non-verbal sounds (like groans or screams) or written text shown on screen.

variance_decomposition.definition: The cognitive complexity of the spoken language, requiring the listener (and viewer) to hold complex verbal information, technical terms, or lists in working memory.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: reciting a long list of numbers or coordinates; explaining a highly complex scientific or technical theory; giving intricate, multi-step verbal directions; using dense, obscure legal or medical jargon; spelling out a long, complicated word or code
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for technical jargon, numbers, codes, lists, complex instructions, or highly abstract concepts spoken aloud.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score emotionally complex dialogue that is linguistically simple, nor visual complexity that is not spoken.

variance_decomposition.definition: Detailed visual inspection of specific non-face objects requiring subordinate-level categorization or expert discrimination (e.g., identifying a specific car model, antique, or tool).
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: examining a rare coin; inspecting a vintage car; identifying a specific tool; analyzing an antique; close-up of a complex mechanism
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on the level of detail, close-up framing, and expert-level discrimination applied to non-face objects.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score high for general object presence; the object must be inspected at a subordinate, detailed level.

variance_decomposition.definition: The focus on physical touch, textures, temperature extremes, skin-to-skin contact, or intense somatic sensations.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: running fingers over a rough, textured stone wall; shivering violently in a freezing cold blizzard; a gentle, intimate caress of a character's cheek; the feeling of heavy rain soaking through clothing; submerging a hand into thick, sticky mud
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for descriptions of textures (rough, smooth, sticky), temperatures (hot, freezing), and the physical sensation of touch or skin contact.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score visual descriptions of objects unless their tactile qualities are explicitly emphasized or experienced by a character.

variance_decomposition.definition: Detailed visual inspection of specific non-face objects requiring subordinate-level categorization or expert discrimination (e.g., identifying a specific car model, antique, or tool).
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: examining a rare coin; inspecting a vintage car; identifying a specific tool; analyzing an antique; close-up of a complex mechanism
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on the level of detail, close-up framing, and expert-level discrimination applied to non-face objects.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score high for general object presence; the object must be inspected at a subordinate, detailed level.

variance_decomposition.definition: Inferred feeling of lighthearted humor, playfulness, or laughter triggered by funny, absurd, or witty narrative elements.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A character making a witty joke or sarcastic remark.; A slapstick or physically comical situation.; An absurd, ironic, or unexpected comedic turn of events.
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for explicit jokes, witty banter, physical comedy, or absurd situations described in the text.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score general happiness, joy, or relief unless there is a clear element of humor or playfulness.

variance_decomposition.definition: Inferred feeling of lighthearted humor, playfulness, or laughter triggered by funny, absurd, or witty narrative elements.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A character making a witty joke or sarcastic remark.; A slapstick or physically comical situation.; An absurd, ironic, or unexpected comedic turn of events.
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for explicit jokes, witty banter, physical comedy, or absurd situations described in the text.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score general happiness, joy, or relief unless there is a clear element of humor or playfulness.

variance_decomposition.definition: The physical manner of walking, running, or moving through space, conveying intent, urgency, or physical state (e.g., sneaking, stomping, stumbling).
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: Sneaking stealthily on tiptoes; Stumbling or limping heavily due to injury; Sprinting frantically away from danger; Stomping angrily out of a room
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on the style, speed, urgency, and physical effort involved in a character's walking, running, or physical movement through space.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score passive transportation (like riding in a car) or stationary physical actions.

variance_decomposition.definition: The focus on physical touch, textures, temperature extremes, skin-to-skin contact, or intense somatic sensations.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: running fingers over a rough, textured stone wall; shivering violently in a freezing cold blizzard; a gentle, intimate caress of a character's cheek; the feeling of heavy rain soaking through clothing; submerging a hand into thick, sticky mud
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for descriptions of textures (rough, smooth, sticky), temperatures (hot, freezing), and the physical sensation of touch or skin contact.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score visual descriptions of objects unless their tactile qualities are explicitly emphasized or experienced by a character.

variance_decomposition.definition: The focus on physical touch, textures, temperature extremes, skin-to-skin contact, or intense somatic sensations.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: running fingers over a rough, textured stone wall; shivering violently in a freezing cold blizzard; a gentle, intimate caress of a character's cheek; the feeling of heavy rain soaking through clothing; submerging a hand into thick, sticky mud
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for descriptions of textures (rough, smooth, sticky), temperatures (hot, freezing), and the physical sensation of touch or skin contact.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score visual descriptions of objects unless their tactile qualities are explicitly emphasized or experienced by a character.

variance_decomposition.definition: The cognitive complexity of the spoken language, requiring the listener (and viewer) to hold complex verbal information, technical terms, or lists in working memory.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: reciting a long list of numbers or coordinates; explaining a highly complex scientific or technical theory; giving intricate, multi-step verbal directions; using dense, obscure legal or medical jargon; spelling out a long, complicated word or code
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for technical jargon, numbers, codes, lists, complex instructions, or highly abstract concepts spoken aloud.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score emotionally complex dialogue that is linguistically simple, nor visual complexity that is not spoken.

variance_decomposition.definition: The focus on physical touch, textures, temperature extremes, skin-to-skin contact, or intense somatic sensations.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: running fingers over a rough, textured stone wall; shivering violently in a freezing cold blizzard; a gentle, intimate caress of a character's cheek; the feeling of heavy rain soaking through clothing; submerging a hand into thick, sticky mud
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for descriptions of textures (rough, smooth, sticky), temperatures (hot, freezing), and the physical sensation of touch or skin contact.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score visual descriptions of objects unless their tactile qualities are explicitly emphasized or experienced by a character.

variance_decomposition.definition: The degree to which the text describes a clear shift in the narrative situation, such as a change in time, location, or a major plot transition, requiring the viewer to establish a new situation model.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A sudden jump forward or backward in time; A transition to a completely new location; A major shift in the focus of the plot; An abrupt interruption of the current scene by a new event
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for explicit indicators of time jumps, location changes, or sudden plot twists in the text.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score gradual, continuous movements within the same scene or minor actions that do not disrupt the overall situation model.

variance_decomposition.definition: The density, speed, and overall tempo of spoken dialogue or narration in the scene.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: characters speaking rapidly and overlapping; a fast-paced, witty banter sequence; a rapid-fire monologue or voiceover; characters interrupting each other constantly; a high volume of spoken words in a short period
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on the described speed, density, and frequency of spoken words, dialogue exchanges, or voiceovers.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score non-verbal sounds (like groans or screams) or written text shown on screen.

variance_decomposition.definition: The degree to which the viewer must infer what a character believes to be true, or what knowledge they possess or lack, especially when their beliefs are false or outdated.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A character acting on a false belief; A character realizing they were mistaken; A character searching for information they lack
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for descriptions of characters acting on incomplete information, expressing mistaken assumptions, or discovering new facts.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score simple emotional reactions or physical actions that do not require tracking what a character knows or believes.

variance_decomposition.definition: Inferred feeling of lighthearted humor, playfulness, or laughter triggered by funny, absurd, or witty narrative elements.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A character making a witty joke or sarcastic remark.; A slapstick or physically comical situation.; An absurd, ironic, or unexpected comedic turn of events.
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for explicit jokes, witty banter, physical comedy, or absurd situations described in the text.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score general happiness, joy, or relief unless there is a clear element of humor or playfulness.

variance_decomposition.definition: Inferred feeling of lighthearted humor, playfulness, or laughter triggered by funny, absurd, or witty narrative elements.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A character making a witty joke or sarcastic remark.; A slapstick or physically comical situation.; An absurd, ironic, or unexpected comedic turn of events.
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for explicit jokes, witty banter, physical comedy, or absurd situations described in the text.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score general happiness, joy, or relief unless there is a clear element of humor or playfulness.

variance_decomposition.definition: Inferred feeling of lighthearted humor, playfulness, or laughter triggered by funny, absurd, or witty narrative elements.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A character making a witty joke or sarcastic remark.; A slapstick or physically comical situation.; An absurd, ironic, or unexpected comedic turn of events.
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for explicit jokes, witty banter, physical comedy, or absurd situations described in the text.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score general happiness, joy, or relief unless there is a clear element of humor or playfulness.

variance_decomposition.definition: The cognitive complexity of the spoken language, requiring the listener (and viewer) to hold complex verbal information, technical terms, or lists in working memory.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: reciting a long list of numbers or coordinates; explaining a highly complex scientific or technical theory; giving intricate, multi-step verbal directions; using dense, obscure legal or medical jargon; spelling out a long, complicated word or code
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for technical jargon, numbers, codes, lists, complex instructions, or highly abstract concepts spoken aloud.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score emotionally complex dialogue that is linguistically simple, nor visual complexity that is not spoken.

variance_decomposition.definition: The density, speed, and overall tempo of spoken dialogue or narration in the scene.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: characters speaking rapidly and overlapping; a fast-paced, witty banter sequence; a rapid-fire monologue or voiceover; characters interrupting each other constantly; a high volume of spoken words in a short period
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on the described speed, density, and frequency of spoken words, dialogue exchanges, or voiceovers.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score non-verbal sounds (like groans or screams) or written text shown on screen.

variance_decomposition.definition: The physical manner of walking, running, or moving through space, conveying intent, urgency, or physical state (e.g., sneaking, stomping, stumbling).
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: Sneaking stealthily on tiptoes; Stumbling or limping heavily due to injury; Sprinting frantically away from danger; Stomping angrily out of a room
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on the style, speed, urgency, and physical effort involved in a character's walking, running, or physical movement through space.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score passive transportation (like riding in a car) or stationary physical actions.

variance_decomposition.definition: Reading between the lines of dialogue to decode unspoken tension, sarcasm, double entendres, coded language, or implicit threats.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: Sarcastic or passive-aggressive remarks; Coded language or metaphors used to discuss a forbidden topic; Unspoken romantic or hostile tension beneath polite conversation; Double meanings where a statement has a hidden significance for certain characters
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Driven by descriptions of passive-aggressive dialogue, coded speech, double meanings, and the contrast between literal words and emotional undertones.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score direct, open arguments or explicit threats where the conflict is fully out in the open.

variance_decomposition.definition: Inferring characters' hidden thoughts, beliefs, knowledge states, or emotional perspectives.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: realizing someone is lying; wondering what another thinks; misunderstanding someone's intent; empathizing with a secret struggle
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on how much the text requires or describes the tracking of internal mental states of others.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score simple descriptions of basic emotions (e.g., 'he was sad') without cognitive perspective-taking.

variance_decomposition.definition: The complexity of the communication between characters, focusing on subtext, non-verbal social cues, sarcasm, or reading between the lines of a conversation.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A character using heavy sarcasm or irony; A conversation filled with unspoken subtext; Characters communicating through subtle non-verbal cues
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for descriptions of sarcasm, flirting, coded language, tense silences, meaningful glances, and conversations where characters are hiding their true thoughts behind polite words.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score loud, direct shouting matches where there is no subtext and everything is expressed explicitly.

variance_decomposition.definition: Reading between the lines of dialogue to decode unspoken tension, sarcasm, double entendres, coded language, or implicit threats.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: Sarcastic or passive-aggressive remarks; Coded language or metaphors used to discuss a forbidden topic; Unspoken romantic or hostile tension beneath polite conversation; Double meanings where a statement has a hidden significance for certain characters
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Driven by descriptions of passive-aggressive dialogue, coded speech, double meanings, and the contrast between literal words and emotional undertones.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score direct, open arguments or explicit threats where the conflict is fully out in the open.

variance_decomposition.definition: The degree to which the current scene requires the viewer to connect ongoing events to prior plot points, tracking causal chains across the narrative.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A character referencing a past event that explains their current action.; The reveal of a connection between two previously unrelated plot threads.; An event occurring as a direct, long-awaited consequence of an earlier scene.
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for text describing clues, revelations, callbacks, or consequences that require the viewer to retrieve and integrate information from earlier in the movie.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score simple chronological progression (e.g., 'then he walked to the next room') unless it involves integrating non-adjacent narrative information.

variance_decomposition.definition: The degree to which the current scene requires the viewer to connect ongoing events to prior plot points, tracking causal chains across the narrative.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A character referencing a past event that explains their current action.; The reveal of a connection between two previously unrelated plot threads.; An event occurring as a direct, long-awaited consequence of an earlier scene.
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for text describing clues, revelations, callbacks, or consequences that require the viewer to retrieve and integrate information from earlier in the movie.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score simple chronological progression (e.g., 'then he walked to the next room') unless it involves integrating non-adjacent narrative information.

variance_decomposition.definition: The degree to which the current scene requires the viewer to connect ongoing events to prior plot points, tracking causal chains across the narrative.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A character referencing a past event that explains their current action.; The reveal of a connection between two previously unrelated plot threads.; An event occurring as a direct, long-awaited consequence of an earlier scene.
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for text describing clues, revelations, callbacks, or consequences that require the viewer to retrieve and integrate information from earlier in the movie.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score simple chronological progression (e.g., 'then he walked to the next room') unless it involves integrating non-adjacent narrative information.

variance_decomposition.definition: Connecting current events to past occurrences or future consequences in the plot to maintain narrative coherence.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: recalling a past clue; realizing the consequence of an earlier action; connecting two seemingly unrelated events; foreshadowing a future event
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on how much the scene requires the viewer to integrate information across different times or scenes.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score simple chronological progressions without causal connections (e.g., 'then he went to bed').

variance_decomposition.definition: Connecting current events to past occurrences or future consequences in the plot to maintain narrative coherence.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: recalling a past clue; realizing the consequence of an earlier action; connecting two seemingly unrelated events; foreshadowing a future event
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on how much the scene requires the viewer to integrate information across different times or scenes.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score simple chronological progressions without causal connections (e.g., 'then he went to bed').

variance_decomposition.definition: The focus on physical touch, textures, temperature extremes, skin-to-skin contact, or intense somatic sensations.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: running fingers over a rough, textured stone wall; shivering violently in a freezing cold blizzard; a gentle, intimate caress of a character's cheek; the feeling of heavy rain soaking through clothing; submerging a hand into thick, sticky mud
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for descriptions of textures (rough, smooth, sticky), temperatures (hot, freezing), and the physical sensation of touch or skin contact.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score visual descriptions of objects unless their tactile qualities are explicitly emphasized or experienced by a character.

variance_decomposition.definition: The focus on physical touch, textures, temperature extremes, skin-to-skin contact, or intense somatic sensations.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: running fingers over a rough, textured stone wall; shivering violently in a freezing cold blizzard; a gentle, intimate caress of a character's cheek; the feeling of heavy rain soaking through clothing; submerging a hand into thick, sticky mud
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for descriptions of textures (rough, smooth, sticky), temperatures (hot, freezing), and the physical sensation of touch or skin contact.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score visual descriptions of objects unless their tactile qualities are explicitly emphasized or experienced by a character.

variance_decomposition.definition: The cognitive demand of updating the overall mental model of the story's situation, characters, or rules based on new, significant information.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: A sudden plot twist that changes the understanding of a character's identity.; The introduction of a major new rule or condition in the narrative world.; A revelation that a previously assumed fact was false.
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Driven by text describing revelations, twists, unexpected discoveries, or paradigm shifts that force the viewer to change their understanding of the story's reality.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score simple visual surprises or jump scares that do not alter the conceptual understanding of the narrative.

variance_decomposition.definition: The focus on physical touch, textures, temperature extremes, skin-to-skin contact, or intense somatic sensations.
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: running fingers over a rough, textured stone wall; shivering violently in a freezing cold blizzard; a gentle, intimate caress of a character's cheek; the feeling of heavy rain soaking through clothing; submerging a hand into thick, sticky mud
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Look for descriptions of textures (rough, smooth, sticky), temperatures (hot, freezing), and the physical sensation of touch or skin contact.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score visual descriptions of objects unless their tactile qualities are explicitly emphasized or experienced by a character.

variance_decomposition.definition: Detailed visual inspection of specific non-face objects requiring subordinate-level categorization or expert discrimination (e.g., identifying a specific car model, antique, or tool).
variance_decomposition.trigger_list: examining a rare coin; inspecting a vintage car; identifying a specific tool; analyzing an antique; close-up of a complex mechanism
variance_decomposition.scoreability_note: Score based on the level of detail, close-up framing, and expert-level discrimination applied to non-face objects.
variance_decomposition.exclusion_note: Do not score high for general object presence; the object must be inspected at a subordinate, detailed level.

representative_test_examples.source_description: Ross points a finger toward Phoebe and says, "I see what this is. You are in love with Rachel." Phoebe looks at him.
representative_test_examples.definition: The density, speed, and overall tempo of spoken dialogue or narration in the scene.

representative_test_examples.source_description: Phoebe turns her head back toward the woman at the desk and says, "Well, maybe you wouldn't have if you could run in the chapel."
representative_test_examples.definition: The degree to which the viewer must evaluate a character's level of blame, intentionality, or negligence behind a harmful outcome, distinguishing between accidents, negligence, and deliberate malice.

representative_test_examples.source_description: Chandler continues, "We have been playing these babies man to man. We" as he gestures with the baby bottle in his right hand.
representative_test_examples.definition: The density, speed, and overall tempo of spoken dialogue or narration in the scene.

representative_test_examples.source_description: Chandler looks at his own cards and says, "I have two queens. What do you have?"
representative_test_examples.definition: The degree to which the scene features active, back-and-forth verbal communication and conversational turn-taking between characters.

representative_test_examples.source_description: Chandler says, "Well, I was trying to prove that I was right. And turns out I was wrong. And now it's lodged my throat." He gestures with his hand toward his neck.
representative_test_examples.definition: The density, speed, and overall tempo of spoken dialogue or narration in the scene.

notes: Exploratory report for non-final round8 encoding.
Ridge coefficients are standardized linear prediction weights, not causal effects.
Variance decomposition uses Cov(X_j beta_j, yhat) / Var(yhat) in standardized prediction space.
Cross-schema contribution refers to feature schema source, not neural causal influence.
